chunk_summary: Outpatient first-week plan for adolescents with anorexia nervosa or atypical anorexia includes medical monitoring, parent-led meal supervision, and criteria for escalation to inpatient care.
learning_objectives: Understand the initial outpatient management plan for adolescents with anorexia nervosa or atypical anorexia.
Identify key medical assessments and monitoring protocols during the first week of outpatient care.
Recognize criteria for escalation to inpatient care based on clinical and physiological parameters.
key_facts: Day 0 assessments include standardized weight, orthostatic vitals, baseline labs (electrolytes, glucose, LFT), and ECG if indicated.
Parent-led meal supervision involves three meals and 2–3 snacks daily, with caregivers plating meals to relieve adolescents of portion decisions.
Twice-weekly medical reviews are recommended in week 1 for low weight, borderline vitals, or unreliable intake; weekly reviews are the minimum once weight restoration begins.
Escalation to inpatient care is required for <75% median BMI, dehydration, hypophosphatemia, prolonged QTc, severe bradycardia, or other critical symptoms.
Orthostatic changes indicating concern include a pulse increase >20/min or BP drop >20/10 mmHg.
reference_quote: Start parent-led meal supervision immediately: three meals plus 2–3 snacks daily, with caregivers plating meals so the adolescent is relieved of portion decisions.
